_meta.how_to_regress: npm run check:golden task-avalanche-breaker  (dev backend up)
_meta.note: case guards habit_stack wiring (was end-to-end no-op) + clean estimated_time values.

input.project: Clean out and reorganize the garage before winter

micro_tasks.task: While finishing your morning coffee on the porch, spend 60 seconds just looking toward the garage and mentally note one thing you'd like out of there.
micro_tasks.why_this_first: Starting with observation instead of action removes the pressure to 'do' anything and eases you in gently.
micro_tasks.habit_stack: As you take your last few sips of morning coffee on the porch, let your eyes drift to the garage door and simply think — no action required yet.
micro_tasks.completion_criteria: Done when you've identified one item or area in the garage that bothers you most.
micro_tasks.if_stuck: Just stand in front of the closed garage door for 30 seconds — that still counts.

micro_tasks.task: Open the garage door and grab one obviously-trash item — a single empty box, broken item, or piece of trash — and throw it away.
micro_tasks.why_this_first: One small win creates physical proof that progress is happening, which makes continuing easier.
micro_tasks.completion_criteria: Done when one piece of trash or junk is in the bin.
micro_tasks.if_stuck: Just walk in, look down, grab the first trash-looking thing you see without thinking.

micro_tasks.task: Walk slowly through the garage for 90 seconds and mentally sort what you see into three loose buckets: trash, keep, and 'deal with later.'
micro_tasks.why_this_first: A quick mental map prevents random grabbing and makes the next physical tasks feel guided rather than chaotic.
micro_tasks.completion_criteria: Done when you've walked the full space at least once and have a rough sense of what's there.
micro_tasks.if_stuck: Just stand in the middle and slowly turn in a circle — even that gives you information.

micro_tasks.task: Collect all visible loose trash (wrappers, bags, broken bits) into one trash bag without moving anything big.
micro_tasks.completion_criteria: Done when surface-level trash is bagged and ready to take out.
micro_tasks.if_stuck: Grab just three pieces of trash — that's still a win.

micro_tasks.task: Pick one small category of items already clustered together — such as sports gear, tools, or garden supplies — and move them all to one spot.
micro_tasks.completion_criteria: Done when one category of items is grouped together in a single location.
micro_tasks.if_stuck: Just push two similar items next to each other — grouping has begun.

micro_tasks.task: Snap one photo of the garage from the doorway so you have a 'before' reference for next time.
micro_tasks.completion_criteria: Done when one photo is saved on your phone.
micro_tasks.if_stuck: Skip it — this is optional and only useful if you'd find it motivating later.

micro_tasks.task: Write down — or voice-memo — the single most important thing to tackle in the next garage session so you don't have to re-figure it out next time.
micro_tasks.completion_criteria: Done when one specific next-session priority is recorded somewhere you'll find it.
micro_tasks.if_stuck: Just say it out loud once — even unrecorded, it plants the seed in your memory.

anti_paralysis_strategies.if_cant_start: Do ONLY task 1 — sit with your coffee and glance at the garage, nothing more.
anti_paralysis_strategies.if_decision_paralysis: Pick the first thing your eyes land on and move it; deciding perfectly matters less than deciding at all.
anti_paralysis_strategies.permission_to_stop: Stop after any task and call it a win — every minute you spent today is a minute the garage didn't get worse.

momentum_checkpoints.celebration: You physically touched the garage — that invisible wall is broken!
You cleared trash AND created one organized zone — that's real, visible progress!
